summary: Large fighter subsystem decompilation PR that replaced asm/melee/ft/fighter.s with a 3373-line src/melee/ft/fighter.c, expanded fighter.h, and propagated a newly named Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC callsite symbol across fighter assembly and C files. The PR also added per-object Makefile flags for fighter.c, introduced an m2ctx.py context-generation helper, and adjusted global function/variable declarations needed by the new C translation. The PR body only says it allowed automerge and there were no review comments, so intent beyond finishing fighter.c is inferred from the diff.
what_changed: Deleted the old 7057-line asm/melee/ft/fighter.s body and added src/melee/ft/fighter.c with 3372 new lines, completing the C-side replacement for that translation unit.

Added many fighter.c globals and externs, including ft_OnLoad[33], ft_OnDeath[33], ft_OnAbsorb[33], lbl_803C10D0[33], lbl_803C0EC0[33], lbl_804598B8[33], HSD_PadRumbleData[4], stage_info, lbl_80458FD0, lbl_80458FFC, lbl_804D64F8, and p_ftCommonData.

Implemented or moved numerous fighter core functions into C, including func_800679B0, func_80067A84, func_80067ABC, func_80067BB4, func_80067C98, func_80068354, func_80068E98, Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC, Fighter_procUpdate, Fighter_TakeDamage_8006CC7C, and surrounding func_8006xxxx routines.

Renamed/prototyped func_800693AC as Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC and updated callsites across many ft asm files and C files, including Crazy Hand, Donkey, Kirby, code_800877F8.s, ftcommon.s, ftcoll.s, Marth specials, Koopa, Mario, Clink, Sandbag, ftlib, ftwalljump, player, and include/functions.h.
Expanded src/melee/ft/fighter.h substantially, with 287 added and 160 deleted lines, to support the new C implementation and cross-file declarations.
Added a Makefile object-specific compile override: src/melee/ft/fighter.c.o uses CFLAGS += -inline noauto and CC_EPI := $(CC).
Added src/m2ctx.py, a GCC-preprocessor-based context generator for mips_to_c/decomp work, using project include paths and M2CTX-specific preprocessor defines.
Removed fighter.s from obj_files.mk, consistent with replacing the asm object with src/melee/ft/fighter.c.
smart_moves: Centralized the action-state-change symbol under the descriptive name Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC, then mechanically updated hundreds of dependent callsites so mixed asm/C users refer to the same symbol.
Used a targeted Makefile override for only fighter.c instead of changing global compiler behavior; -inline noauto is explicitly scoped to the problematic translation unit.

Preserved uncertainty in comments instead of over-renaming: examples include TODOs around NUM_FIGHTERS versus hardcoded 33, verifying g_spawnNumCounter, uncertain HSD_ObjAllocInit sizes, and data still duplicated with variables.h.

Kept address-suffixed names for unclear functions while adding semantic names where the evidence was stronger, such as Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC, Fighter_procUpdate, and Fighter_TakeDamage_8006CC7C.
Added a reusable context-generation script rather than relying only on ad hoc scratch contexts; this helps future mips_to_c iterations reproduce include and macro setup.
Documented matching state inline for some functions with decomp.me scratch links and comments such as Matches or No Match, making future cleanup easier to audit.
decomp_lessons: For very large core files, a successful asm-to-C replacement may require broad symbol propagation through still-asm character files; renaming a common API should be synchronized across asm branches, C files, and include/functions.h.

Hardcoded array sizes like 33 can be acceptable as an interim decomp step when backed by comments, but future cleanup should replace them with a named fighter-count constant only after loops and data tables are understood.
Object-specific compiler flags are a useful matching tactic for one troublesome file; avoid global flag changes when one TU needs -inline noauto.
When migrating data out of asm, keep notes about duplicated or not-yet-moved variables to avoid conflicting definitions between source files and variables.h.
Retaining decomp.me scratch links and match-status comments in a large landing PR gives later contributors a breadcrumb trail for remaining nonmatching or fragile functions.

For C/asm interop, function prototypes must use compatible types across HSD_GObj, Fighter, f32, s32, and NULL arguments; the PR shows callsite cleanup from func_800693AC(..., 0, ...) to Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC(..., NULL, ...).
naming_conventions: Semantic fighter API names use a Fighter_ prefix while retaining the address suffix for traceability, e.g. Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC and Fighter_TakeDamage_8006CC7C.
Unresolved symbols remain in lbl_XXXXXXXX or func_XXXXXXXX form until their role is certain.

Local aliases can bridge unclear globals to useful names, as in #define g_debugLevel lbl_804D4A08 and #define g_spawnNumCounter lbl_804D64F8, but comments mark them as TODO/verify rather than final names.

Fighter struct fields retain offset-based names with partial semantic suffixes when known, such as x620_lstick_x, x671_timer_lstick_tilt_y, x108_costume_archive, x5E8_fighterBones, and x4_fighterKind.
Callback arrays use ft_ prefixes for fighter-wide character hooks, including ft_OnLoad, ft_OnDeath, and ft_OnAbsorb.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced the entire fighter.s asm object with C and removed the asm object from obj_files.mk.
Scoped fighter.c compilation to -inline noauto, indicating inlining behavior was important for matching this translation unit.
Added inline helper functions such as fabs_inline and pickValue in fighter.c to shape generated code where needed.
Used explicit unrolled assignments in func_80067ABC to copy PlCo.dat common data into sbss/global slots in reverse order, with comments noting that loop unrolling did not match.
Updated branch labels in asm callsites to the new Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC symbol without changing instruction bytes, preserving asm behavior while improving symbol names.
Used m2ctx.py to preprocess C files into ctx.c with include paths include, src, and ver/current/build/include and defines such as _LANGUAGE_C and M2CTX for mips_to_c contexts.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the PR slice.
The only human text says: "this one is allowing automerge".
There is no direct reviewer rationale in the available evidence; lessons are inferred from the diff and inline comments.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC or remove the address suffix.
Find later PRs that replace hardcoded fighter count 33 with NUM_FIGHTERS or another enum.
Find later PRs that move fighter.c data definitions out of variables.h duplication.
Find later PRs that remove fighter.c's -inline noauto override.
Find later PRs that clean up p_ftCommonData and the lbl_804D64FC..lbl_804D6550 common-data globals.
Find later uses or replacements of src/m2ctx.py and tools/frank.py.
